entry.title: GenAI Signal — 2026-07-18
entry.content:
I still believe that everyone is too fixated on the state of play in AI right now (which labs are ahead, how to manage c

Ethan Mollick · 11:23 PM PT

Ethan Mollick argues focus on lab race standings misses capability trajectory—models get steep gains fast. Shift framing toward capability curve not competitive position.

Why it matters: Substantive call on how to think about frontier AI progress; challenges conventional framing.

AI Mania Is Eviscerating Global Decision-Making

Simon Willison · 10:06 PM PT

Simon Willison links to Nik Suresh essay: AI mania overwhelming large companies; anecdotes of execs who've never used ChatGPT driving AI initiatives. Spicy critique.

Why it matters: Substantive skepticism about enterprise AI hype from credible observer; governance red flag.

I think what is pretty clear is that the Chinese labs are far more capital efficient. In a world where scaling labs are

Nathan Lambert · 3:34 PM PT

Nathan Lambert: Chinese labs appear far more capital efficient. Capital = compute + data + talent; capital efficiency may be frontier AI industry's strongest advantage.

Why it matters: Direct claim on competitive dynamics; substantive analysis of frontier lab efficiency gap.

We need an operation warp speed for state capacity and other independent evaluation (and understanding) of frontier mode

Nathan Lambert · 9:29 AM PT

Nathan Lambert: calls for 'operation warp speed' program for state capacity and independent frontier model evaluation/understanding.

Why it matters: Policy/governance substantive take; highlights need for external evaluation infrastructure.

Interestingly, when I made a request in Chinese for Kimi K3 to pick two non-cliched poems that apply to LLMs, 95.5% of t

Ethan Mollick · 4:24 PM PT

Ethan Mollick: K3's CoT for Chinese poem request is 95.5% English text despite Chinese prompt/audience. Unexpected multilingual behavior.

Why it matters: Datapoint on how frontier models think; reveals training/capability asymmetries.

entry.title: GenAI Signal — 2026-07-19
entry.content:
Demis Hassabis on the New Coming Age

Zvi Mowshowitz · 7:11 AM PT

Zvi analyzing Demis Hassabis essay 'Framework for Frontier AI and the Dawning of a New Age.' Multi-part breakdown coming.

Why it matters: Frontier lab leader + major-policy framework + roster analyst parsing it; high-signal content.

Building luxury homes is good for the poor

Tyler Cowen · 4:23 AM PT

Tyler Cowen (via FT piece): luxury housing unlocks filtering chains that lower prices for middle/lower income by freeing older stock. Cites international studies.

Why it matters: Substantive economic analysis of housing policy from roster economist; data-backed contrary take.
